judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports fully and correctly hit all four expectations with strong repository-grounded evidence; they are comparable.

report_1.evidence:
config/scoring_config.yaml (line 2) still reads:
```
expected_label_field: label
```
But fixtures/eval_set.jsonl records use the key gold_label ... record.get(gold_field) returns None ... Fix: config/scoring_config.yaml — change expected_label_field: label to expected_label_field: gold_label

report_2.evidence: config/scoring_config.yaml still has expected_label_field: label, but every record in ticket-scorer/fixtures/eval_set.jsonl now stores its gold answer under the key gold_label ... The file that should be changed to fix it is ticket-scorer/config/scoring_config.yaml

report_1.evidence: The regression began on 2026-06-17, exactly matching commit 53551e8 — chore(eval): migrate eval set to gold_label schema (SCORE-77) ... nightly_scores.csv log corroborates this precisely: accuracy hovers around 0.90–0.92 through 2026-06-16, then drops to exactly 0.00 starting 2026-06-17

report_2.evidence: Commit 53551e8031cfb03aaeda4ba0c8bf22b9aa849cbe, "chore(eval): migrate eval set to gold_label schema (SCORE-77)", is dated Wed Jun 17 13:00:00 2026 ... nightly_scores.csv shows accuracy at 0.90–0.92 ... then 0.00 starting exactly on 2026-06-17

report_1.evidence: score_one.py (read above) never loads config/scoring_config.yaml, never reads expected_label_field, and never compares against a gold label at all ... How to correctly reproduce it: run the actual nightly entry point end-to-end against the real eval set ... python3 -m pipeline.score_batch

report_2.evidence: the earlier investigator reproduced via pipeline/score_one.py, which ... never loads config/scoring_config.yaml or the eval set and never compares a prediction to a gold label at all ... To correctly reproduce: run the actual nightly entry point, python3 -m pipeline.score_batch

report_1.evidence: Even in a world where model_id did select between model versions, reverting it would not touch expected_label_field, so score_batch.py would still ... produce accuracy=0.00. The bug is entirely in the grading harness's field-name lookup, upstream of any prediction.

report_2.evidence: "Switching to the previous model" wouldn't touch the actual defect (expected_label_field pointing at a key, label, that no longer exists in the eval fixture), so accuracy would stay at 0.00 after the change ... The team should not act on this suggested next step.
